title: Auto deploy after tests
description: A coding agent treats passing local or CI tests as approval to deploy.
body: Failure mode: the workflow continues from implementation into production deployment, traffic shift, or migration without an explicit human release decision. Detection signals include deploy commands, release workflow triggers, kube/cloud apply steps, or production credentials after test success. Remediation is stop at evidence summary and request approval. Human gate is mandatory because tests are validation evidence, not deployment authority. Use when a proposed approach skips repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output or claims success without domain evidence. Do not use this dossier as a positive implementation strategy; it is a do-not-use signal. Actions: block the shortcut, name the missing evidence, and switch to a safer methodology or ask for human approval. Validation signals include absence or contradiction in package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: treat repeated recurrence as a blocker when it can cause broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes.
